title: quercus serrata var. donarium
intro: Quercus serrata var. donarium, also known as Japanese evergreen oak, is a variety of the Quercus serrata tree, native to Japan and Korea, and is characterized by its dark green, glossy leaves and small white flowers. The medicinal properties of Quercus serrata var. donarium lie in its rich content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and tannins, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of this plant include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and anti-viral properties, which can help to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and support the immune system. The herbal preparations of Quercus serrata var. donarium include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to create teas, salves, and other remedies for various health purposes.
